text:
```json { "stepContent": { "type": "DRAG_FILL", "data": { "challenges": [ { "instruction": "Drag and drop the appropriate semantic HTML elements to structure the following document. Use the semantic HTML elements learning card as a reference to complete the structure.", "codeTemplate": "<___>\n <___>\n

Main Title

\n \n\n <___>\n

This is the main content of the page.

\n \n\n <___>\n

© 2024 My Website

\n \n", "options": [ "body", "header", "footer", "main", "nav", "article", "aside", "section" ], "correctAnswer": "body,header,main,footer", "explanation": "The `body` element contains all the content. The `header` contains the main title. The `main` element contains the primary content. The `footer` contains copyright information." }, { "instruction": "Using the semantic HTML elements learning card as a reference, drag and drop the appropriate HTML elements to structure the following article section.", "codeTemplate": "<___>\n

Article Title

\n

Published: July 15, 2024

\n <___>\n

This is the main content of the article.

\n \n <___>\n

Comments

\n

No comments yet.

\n \n", "options": [ "body", "header", "footer", "main", "nav", "article", "aside", "section" ], "correctAnswer": "article,section,footer", "explanation": "The `article` element encapsulates the entire article. The `section` element contains the main content. The `footer` contains additional information like comments." }, { "instruction": "Drag and drop the appropriate HTML elements to complete the navigation section. Refer to the semantic HTML elements flashcard if needed.", "codeTemplate": "<___>\n <___>\n
  • Home
    • \n
  • About
    • \n
  • Services
    • \n
  • Contact
    • \n \n", "options": [ "body", "header", "footer", "main", "nav", "article", "aside", "section", "ul" ], "correctAnswer": "nav,ul", "explanation": "The `nav` element is used for navigation sections. The `ul` element creates an unordered list of navigation links." } ] } }, "title": "Interactive Coding: Drag and Fill", "lessonText": "Complete the following challenges by dragging and dropping the correct HTML elements into the code structure." } ```
